message:
fix: PR #6503 aftermath — concurrency races, storage comment, feedback fetch, test (#6517, #6518) (#6552)

Addresses the 8 Copilot comments on PR #6503 (wave7) plus the
FeatureRequestButton test failure introduced by issue #6475.

Go concurrency (pkg/k8s/client.go):
  A. StartWatching: hold the lock across the check-and-set of
     m.watching so two concurrent callers cannot both construct a
     fresh fsnotify.Watcher and leak the loser. Roll back the
     reservation on construction/add errors.
  B. StopWatching: call stopWatchOnce.Do while still holding m.mu so a
     concurrent Stop->Start that rotates m.stopWatchOnce cannot race
     with an in-flight Do on the old Once.
  H. reloadAndNotify: acquire m.mu before reading/mutating m.watcher,
     and skip the re-watch entirely when watching=false so a
     debounced timer firing after Stop does not touch a closed
     watcher.

  Added two race-detector tests in watcher_lifecycle_test.go:
    - TestMultiClusterClient_StartWatching_ConcurrentRace (100 goroutines)
    - TestMultiClusterClient_StopStartRace (interleaved Stop/Start cycles)

Frontend / docs / test:
  C. web/package.json: test:e2e now runs "npm run build && playwright
     test" so Go prod server has ./web/dist to serve.
  D. useMissionControl.ts: fix PERSIST_STATE_DEBOUNCE_MS comment to say
     localStorage (persistState writes localStorage via STORAGE_KEY).
  E. Missions.spec.ts: correct the setupMissionsTest() route-override
     comment — the project-card test must unroute AFTER setup, not
     before; the original "override BEFORE setupMissionsTest()" note
     described an impossible ordering.
  F. MissionBrowser.tsx: wrap selectCardMission in useCallback (stable,
     empty deps — state setters are stable, fetchMissionContent is a
     module import) and add it to the deep-link effect's deps, fixing
     the exhaustive-deps warning without thrashing.
  G. Add ?count_only=true support to /api/feedback/queue and
     useFeatureRequests (new UseFeatureRequestsOptions). The navbar
     FeatureRequestButton only needs closed-request IDs to filter
     notifications for the badge count, so it now requests the lean
     payload on every page load instead of the full queue with titles,
     bodies, PR URLs, etc. Full queue consumers (Updates tab, modal)
     are unchanged.

#6517 — FeatureRequestButton "renders without crashing" test:
  The mock in FeatureRequestButton.test.tsx only exported
  useNotifications; #6475 added a useFeatureRequests consumer so the
  real hook ran, hit api.get, and crashed under vitest. Mock now
  exposes useFeatureRequests: () => ({ requests: [] }) and the matching
  notifications shape.

Verification:
  - go build ./... / go vet ./... clean
  - go test ./pkg/k8s/ -race -timeout 180s PASS (incl. new race tests)
  - cd web && npm run build PASS
  - npx vitest run src/components/feedback src/components/missions
      src/components/mission-control src/test/ui-ux-standards.test.ts
      -> 74/74 PASS
  - npx playwright test --list registers normally
  - npm run lint: identical 1062 problems (330/732) before and after,
    zero new errors or warnings

Fixes #6517
Fixes #6518

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -77,12 +77,18 @@ async function setupMissionsTest(page: Page) {
 
   // Mock GitHub mission listings used by the missions browser. An empty list
   // is fine for the dialog-renders test. Tests that need specific mission data
-  // must override this BEFORE setupMissionsTest() runs by using page.unroute()
-  // then re-registering — see the "project card" test below.
+  // must override this AFTER setupMissionsTest() runs by calling
+  // page.unroute('**/api/missions/list**') to drop this default handler, then
+  // registering a fresh page.route() with the desired response — see the
+  // "project card" test below.
   //
-  // #6474 — Previously we registered a second route handler inline in the
-  // specific test; the second registration does not override the first, so the
-  // empty-list handler won that race and the project-card test was dead.
+  // #6474 / PR #6518 item E — Previously we registered a second route handler
+  // inline in the specific test without unroute(); the second registration
+  // does NOT override the first — Playwright stacks handlers and matches in
+  // order, so the empty-list handler won that race and the project-card test
+  // was dead. Earlier comment incorrectly said "override BEFORE
+  // setupMissionsTest()" which was impossible (setupMissionsTest runs in
+  // beforeEach, before any test body).
   await page.route('**/api/missions/list**', (route) =>
     route.fulfill({
       status: 200,

@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@
     "test": "vitest",
     "test:ui": "vitest --ui",
     "test:coverage": "vitest --coverage",
-    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
+    "test:e2e": "npm run build && playwright test",
     "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
     "test:e2e:headed": "playwright test --headed",
     "test:e2e:debug": "playwright test --debug",

@@ -101,7 +101,10 @@ export function buildInstallPromptForProject(
  * projects appear within one frame of the stream pausing.
  */
 const STREAM_JSON_DEBOUNCE_MS = 250
-/** #6468 — sessionStorage persist debounce window (ms). Coalesces bursts of state changes. */
+/** #6468 — localStorage persist debounce window (ms). Coalesces bursts of
+ * state changes before calling persistState(), which writes to localStorage
+ * (see STORAGE_KEY usage below). Earlier revision of this comment said
+ * "sessionStorage" which is incorrect — fixed in PR #6518 item D. */
 const PERSIST_STATE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 300
 
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
